summary: Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a Nova Poshta facility in Dnipro and announced control of logistics routes deep in occupied Luhansk. Russia’s fuel depot fire in Rostov region prompted evacuations, while the United States tested a new anti‑drone system. Ukraine also received IRIS‑T air‑defence launchers as Russian drone activity intensified across multiple fronts.
key_points: Drone strike destroyed a Nova Poshta building in Dnipro; no casualties reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81570).
Third Army Corps claimed control of logistics routes up to 205 km inside occupied Luhansk, hitting enemy armor and ammo depots (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).

Ukraine received IRIS‑T surface‑to‑air missile launchers; President Zelensky said over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets were launched by Russia in the past week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Multiple Shahed attacks reported near Dnipro, including one near Aviatorske and two near the city (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67304, @mon1tor_ua/67308).
Russian fuel depot fire in Rostov region led to evacuation of nearby private homes; no injuries confirmed (Source: @tass_agency/378316).
US tested the MADIS anti‑drone system using JLTVs equipped with radar, 30‑mm gun and Stinger missiles (Source: @tass_agency/378317).
UAV groups observed over Chernihiv, Rivne, Kherson and other regions moving toward various Ukrainian cities (Source: @kpszsu/63370, @kpszsu/63404).

sections.executive_summary: A Ukrainian drone strike hit a Nova Poshta facility in Dnipro, causing total building loss. The Third Army Corps announced deep‑strike control of logistics routes in occupied Luhansk, destroying enemy armor and ammunition stores. Ukraine received IRIS‑T air‑defence launchers as Russian drone attacks surged across multiple fronts.

points: A hostile drone destroyed a Nova Poshta building in Dnipro; emergency services responded and no personnel were injured (Source: @UkraineNow/81570).

The Third Army Corps reported seizing logistics routes up to 205 km inside occupied Luhansk, striking enemy armored vehicles and ammunition depots, and asserting control over several towns (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).

President Zelensky announced Ukraine’s acquisition of IRIS‑T surface‑to‑air missile launchers and cited Russian use of over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets in the previous week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Multiple Shahed attacks were observed near Dnipro, including one near Aviatorske and two additional contacts near the city (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67304, @mon1tor_ua/67308).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of possible mass attacks and urged vigilance to air‑raid alerts (Source: @UkraineNow/81557).
A fire at a fuel depot in the Rostov region prompted evacuation of nearby private homes; officials reported no casualties (Source: @tass_agency/378316).
Russian authorities reported a UAV attack on the machine hall of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant (Source: @tass_agency/378327).
Russia announced potential symmetric strikes on Ukrainian or NATO nuclear plants if the Zaporizhzhia plant is damaged (Source: @tass_agency/378292).
Russian‑linked Belarusian leader Lukashenko warned that any attack on Belarus would change the character of the war in Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/94443).
Shahed drones were reported flying past Aviatorske and Dnipro, indicating active hostile air activity in the Dnipropetrovsk area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67304, @mon1tor_ua/67308).
Ukrainian forces claimed strikes on logistics routes deep inside occupied Luhansk, targeting supply lines and armored units (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
UAV groups were observed moving from the Black Sea toward Kriviy Rih and from the Kherson‑Mykolaiv border toward Snihurivka (Source: @eRadarrua/94452).
Russian attacks on the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant’s machine hall were reported, raising concerns about nuclear facility security (Source: @tass_agency/378327).
Reactive UAVs were reported along the Belarus‑Ukraine border in northern Chernihiv and northern Kyiv regions (Source: @kpszsu/63401).
UAV activity was noted over Rivne heading toward Rivne and Berizne, and over Chernihiv moving toward Dobryanka (Source: @kpszsu/63371, @eRadarrua/94432).
A threat of tactical aviation activity on the north‑east direction was warned, indicating possible air‑strike preparations (Source: @kpszsu/63373).
UAVs from Zhytomyr were observed heading west toward Rivne (Source: @kpszsu/63406).
UAV groups were reported over the Kherson region moving north‑east toward Nikopol district of Dnipropetrovsk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67312).
Three UAVs were observed near the Mykolaiv‑Kherson border heading toward Snihurivka (Source: @eRadarrua/94452).
UAVs were reported over the Odesa direction from the Black Sea toward Tatarbunary (Source: @kpszsu/63404).
UAV activity was noted in the south‑west of Kherson heading north‑east (Source: @kpszsu/63402).
The United States tested the MADIS anti‑drone system using two JLTVs, one with radar and a 30‑mm gun and the other with Stinger missiles (Source: @tass_agency/378317).
Ukraine reported Russian forces launched over 2,300 drones, nearly 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets in the past week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Multiple Shahed drones were observed near Dnipro and Chernihiv, indicating continued low‑altitude strike attempts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67304, @mon1tor_ua/67307).
UAV groups were reported moving from the Black Sea toward various Ukrainian cities, including Kriviy Rih and Tatarbunary (Source: @kpszsu/63404).

key_developments: A hostile drone destroyed a Nova Poshta building in Dnipro, with emergency crews responding and no injuries reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81570).
The Third Army Corps announced control of logistics routes up to 205 km inside occupied Luhansk, striking enemy armor and ammunition depots (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
Ukraine received IRIS‑T air‑defence launchers as President Zelensky cited Russian use of over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets in the previous week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Multiple Shahed drones were observed near Dnipro, including contacts near Aviatorske and two additional passes over the city (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67304, @mon1tor_ua/67308).
A fire at a fuel depot in Russia’s Rostov region led to evacuation of nearby private homes, with no casualties reported (Source: @tass_agency/378316).
The United States tested the MADIS anti‑drone system, featuring JLTVs equipped with radar, a 30‑mm gun and Stinger missiles (Source: @tass_agency/378317).

UAV groups were reported moving from the Black Sea toward Kriviy Rih and from the Kherson‑Mykolaiv border toward Snihurivka, indicating expanding aerial pressure on southern fronts (Source: @eRadarrua/94452).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are extending operational reach into occupied Luhansk, threatening Russian supply lines, while Russian UAV and missile activity remains high across multiple fronts. The influx of Shahed drones near Dnipro and Chernihiv suggests continued low‑altitude strike tactics. Western anti‑drone technologies, such as MADIS, are being field‑tested, potentially altering the aerial threat balance.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine’s acquisition of IRIS‑T systems and public reporting of high Russian drone sortie rates indicate a push for stronger air defence. Russian evacuations around the Rostov fuel depot show domestic safety concerns, while statements about potential nuclear plant strikes heighten regional tension. Belarusian leadership’s warnings reflect broader regional apprehension about spillover.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV trajectories from the Black Sea and Belarus border is critical for early warning. The reported depth of Ukrainian strikes in Luhansk suggests improved reconnaissance and strike capabilities. Continued observation of Russian statements on nuclear facilities is needed to assess escalation risk.
